description: Risk, compliance, and security service ecosystem — manages fraud detection, access control, audit logging, and regulatory compliance for Omni-Mart.

services.name: Fraud Detection
services.description: Real-time fraud scoring on checkout transactions. Runs ML model inference on transaction features; blocks or flags orders exceeding risk threshold.

services.name: Access Control (AuthZ)
services.description: Centralized authorization service implementing RBAC and ABAC policies for all internal services. Every API call validates permissions through this service.

services.name: Audit Logger
services.description: Immutable append-only audit log for all state-changing operations. Required for SOX, PCI, and internal compliance. Ingests events from all T0/T1 services.

services.name: Identity Provider (IdP)
services.description: SSO and token issuance (JWT/OAuth2). All user and service authentication flows originate here. Outage blocks all authenticated operations.

services.name: Notification Hub
services.description: Multi-channel notification dispatch (email, SMS, push, Slack). Consumes events from various services; delivery is async and eventually consistent.

artifacts.title: Fraud Detection – Service Ownership & SLA
artifacts.content: Fraud Detection is owned by Risk Engineering Team (T0 revenue-critical). On-call: risk-oncall@omnimart.dev. Latency SLA: scoring response < 500ms p99. The model is retrained weekly; model deployment requires Risk Engineering + Data Science sign-off. If Fraud Detection is unavailable, Checkout Orchestrator falls back to rule-based scoring (higher false-positive rate).

artifacts.title: Access Control – Authorization Architecture
artifacts.content: Access Control (AuthZ) implements centralized RBAC with attribute-based extensions. Every service-to-service call passes JWT with scopes; AuthZ validates against policy engine. Deploying AuthZ policy changes requires Platform Security Team review. Blast radius on AuthZ downgrade: all services lose fine-grained permissions — fall back to coarse allow/deny. Blast radius on AuthZ outage: all authenticated API calls fail (platform-wide).

artifacts.title: Audit Logger – Compliance Dependency Map
artifacts.content: Audit Logger receives events from Fraud Detection, Access Control, Checkout Orchestrator, and Payment Gateway via Kafka topic 'audit.events'. Events are written to immutable append-only store (S3 + SQLite index). Compliance Dashboard reads from Audit Logger for SOX and PCI reporting. If Audit Logger is unavailable: no data loss (Kafka retains events), but compliance reporting is delayed. Deploying Audit Logger requires Compliance Team sign-off.

artifacts.title: Identity Provider – Blast Radius Assessment
artifacts.content: Identity Provider (IdP) is the root of all authentication. Downstream: Access Control, Storefront BFF, Mobile BFF, Admin Portal. If IdP is unavailable: no user can log in, no service-to-service token refresh, all authenticated operations blocked. IdP deployment requires Platform Security Team + SRE coordination. Deployment window: off-peak only (< 2 AM ET). Zero-downtime rolling deployment required.

artifacts.title: Fraud Detection – Post-Incident Review (PIR-2026-042)
artifacts.content: Incident: Fraud model produced false negatives for 3 hours after weekly model update. Root cause: training pipeline ingested stale feature set — promotional pricing was not reflected in training data. 47 fraudulent orders were approved. Resolution: added feature-freshness validation in training pipeline; model deployment now requires canary scoring on last 24h transactions with < 0.5% drift threshold before promotion. Lesson: Fraud Detection blast radius extends to financial loss; model deployment is a critical change.
